items.title: Dashboard: distinguish work item types on the board
items.body: Today the HTML dashboard shows stream (plan/emergent), assignee, and optional project badges on cards, but epic vs feature vs story vs task (and bug/chore if present) read similarly. Goal: at-a-glance differentiation (badges, icons, or rail colors) plus a compact legend; detail view should reinforce type. Applies to current embedded-snapshot UI and should carry forward to the fetch-based dashboard.

items.title: Inventory: snapshot item fields vs card/detail rendering for type
items.body: Confirm ItemDto exposes type; list which types exist in schema; note where cardHtml omits type and detail panel shows it.

items.title: UX spec: type chips, legend, and hierarchy cues (epic→task)
items.body: Define per-type label, order, and treatment (badge shape, optional left stripe, icon-free or minimal icon set). Avoid relying on color alone; map to existing theme tokens where possible.

items.title: Board cards: spec markup/CSS classes for each work item type
items.body: Describe card changes (e.g. data-type + class type-epic) and how they interact with stream/status badges for implementation in BuildHtml/JS.

items.title: Detail panel: make type visually primary alongside ref/title
items.body: Short spec for typography or badge in detail header so epic vs task is obvious when a card is selected.

items.title: Accessibility: type labels readable to SR and not color-only
items.body: Checklist: aria text for type, contrast for new badges, keyboard focus unchanged.
